Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kibi no Takehiko}} [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kibi no Takehiko-qq]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]]

'''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient [[Japan]] mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}}
[Founder of the Enō clan]
(Enō region)|02={{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}}
[Founder of the Kibi clan]|03={{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別}}
[Founder of the Kasa clan]
(Namikugi region)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Anehime}}
[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko
[Founder of the Shimotsumichi clan]
(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko
[Founder of the Kamitsu clan]
(Kamitsu region)|03=Otoko
[Founder of the Mino clan]
(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kibi no Takehiko}} [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kibi no Takehiko-qq]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]]

Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}}
[Founder of the Enō clan]
(Enō region)|02={{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}}

[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]

(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko
[Founder of the Kamitsu clan]
(Kamitsu region)|03=Otoko
[Founder of the Mino clan]
(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kibi no Takehiko}} [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kibi no Takehiko-qq]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]]

'''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient [[Japan]] mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}}

After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kibi no Takehiko}} [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kibi no Takehiko-qq]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]]

'''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient [[Japan]] mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}}

[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko

(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko

(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kibi no Takehiko}} [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kibi no Takehiko-qq]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]]

Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kibi no Takehiko}} [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kibi no Takehiko-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]]

'''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient [[Japan]] mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}}
[Founder of the Enō clan]
(Enō region)|02={{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}}
[Founder of the Kibi clan]|03={{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別}}
[Founder of the Kasa clan]
(Namikugi region)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Anehime}}
[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko
[Founder of the Shimotsumichi clan]
(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko
[Founder of the Kamitsu clan]
(Kamitsu region)|03=Otoko
[Founder of the Mino clan]
(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kibi no Takehiko}} [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]]

However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}}
[Founder of the Enō clan]
(Enō region)|02={{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}}
[Founder of the Kibi clan]|03={{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別}}
[Founder of the Kasa clan]
(Namikugi region)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Anehime}}
[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko
[Founder of the Shimotsumichi clan]
(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko
[Founder of the Kamitsu clan]
(Kamitsu region)|03=Otoko
[Founder of the Mino clan]
(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} {{citation needed|Kibi no Takehiko}} [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]]

'''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient [[Japan]] mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}}

It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} {{citation needed|Kibi no Takehiko}} [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]]

revision.text:
'''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient [[Japan]] mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}}

[Founder of the Kasa clan]

[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko

(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} {{citation needed|Kibi no Takehiko}} [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]]

Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} {{citation needed|Kibi no Takehiko}} [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]] [[Category:New]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient [[Japan]] mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}}
[Founder of the Enō clan]
(Enō region)|02={{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}}
[Founder of the Kibi clan]|03={{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別}}
[Founder of the Kasa clan]
(Namikugi region)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Anehime}}
[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko
[Founder of the Shimotsumichi clan]
(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko
[Founder of the Kamitsu clan]
(Kamitsu region)|03=Otoko
[Founder of the Mino clan]
(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} {{citation needed|Kibi no Takehiko}} [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]] [[Category:New]]

'''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient [[Japan]] mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}}
[Founder of the Enō clan]
(Enō region)|02={{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}}
[Founder of the Kibi clan]|03={{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別}}
[Founder of the Kasa clan]
(Namikugi region)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Anehime}}
[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko
[Founder of the Shimotsumichi clan]
(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko
[Founder of the Kamitsu clan]
(Kamitsu region)|03=Otoko
[Founder of the Mino clan]
(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} {{citation needed|Kibi no Takehiko}} [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kibi no Takehiko}}

revision.text:
'''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient [[Japan]] mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別}}
[Founder of the Enō clan]
(Enō region)|02={{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別}}
[Founder of the Kibi clan]|03={{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別}}
[Founder of the Kasa clan]
(Namikugi region)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Anehime}}
[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko
[Founder of the Shimotsumichi clan]
(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko
[Founder of the Kamitsu clan]
(Kamitsu region)|03=Otoko
[Founder of the Mino clan]
(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} {{citation needed|Kibi no Takehiko}} [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kibi no Takehiko}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Kibi no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kibi no Takehiko}}
