revision.text:
'''吉備 武彦'''（きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}）は、『[[日本書紀]]』などに伝わる[[古代日本]]の人物。『日本書紀』では「吉備武彦」、他文献では「吉備武彦命」・「吉備建彦命」とも表記される。 [[景行天皇]]朝における[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]東征の従者の一人とされる{{Sfn|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 系譜 == 吉備武彦の出自について『[[日本書紀]]』に記載はない。『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、左京皇別 下道朝臣条・右京皇別 廬原公条で[[稚武彦命]]（第7代[[孝霊天皇]][[皇子]]）の孫とし、右京皇別 真髪部条では稚武彦命の子とする。 子については、『日本書紀』[[景行天皇]]51年8月4日条において、娘の吉備穴戸武媛が、日本武尊の[[妃]]となって武卵王（たけかいごのきみ）と十城別王（とおきわけのきみ）の2子を産んだと見える{{Sfn|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}。また『日本書紀』[[応神天皇]]22年9月10日条・『[[日本三代実録]]』[[元慶]]3年（[[879年]]）10月22日条では、子として浦凝別（苑臣祖）・[[御友別]]（[[吉備氏|吉備臣]]祖）・[[鴨別]]（笠臣祖）・兄媛（[[応神天皇]]妃）らの名が記されている{{Sfn|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+『[[日本書紀]]』応神天皇22年9月条、『[[日本三代実録]]』元慶3年10月条に基づく関係系図 |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''吉備武彦'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=浦凝別
［苑臣祖］
（苑県に封）|02=[[御友別]]
［[[吉備氏|吉備臣]]祖］|03=[[鴨別]]
［笠臣祖］
（波区芸県に封）|04={{color|#FC4E6B|兄媛}}
（[[応神天皇]]妃）
（織部を賜う）}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=稲速別
［下道臣祖］
（川島県に封）|02=仲彦
［上道臣・香屋臣祖］
（上道県に封）|03=弟彦
［三野臣祖］
（三野県に封）}} {{familytree/end}} |} == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[景行天皇]]40年7月16日条によると、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の東征にあたって、その従者として吉備武彦と[[大伴武日|大伴武日連]]が付けられたという。また同年の是歳条によると、吉備武彦は途中で[[越国]]に視察のため派遣され、のち日本武尊と美濃で合流した。そののち日本武尊が病を得ると、吉備武彦はその遺言を伝える使者として景行天皇の元に遣わされたという{{Sfn|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 関連して『[[古事記]]』景行天皇段では、吉備武彦と同一人物かは定かでないが、吉備臣らの祖の「'''御鉏友耳建日子'''（みすきともみみたけひこ）」が倭建命（日本武尊）の従者として東征に遣わされたことが記されている{{Sfn|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』右京皇別 廬原公条では、景行天皇の時に稚武彦命の孫の「吉備建彦命」は東方に派遣され、毛人を討って「阿倍廬原国」に到ったのち、天皇に復命した日に廬原国を賜ったとする。 == 後裔 == === 氏族 === 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 下道朝臣 - 吉備朝臣同祖。稚武彦命孫の吉備武彦命の後。 * 右京皇別 真髪部 - 稚武彦命男の吉備武彦命の後。 === 国造 === 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」には、次の[[国造]]が後裔として記載されている。 * [[廬原国造]] *: 志賀高穴穂朝（[[成務天皇]]）の御世に池田坂井君祖の吉備武彦命の子の意加部彦命を国造に定める、という。 *: のちの[[駿河国]][[庵原郡]]庵原郷周辺に推定される『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 八木書店、2013年、p. 176。。 == 考証 == 『日本書紀』応神天皇22年9月条と『日本三代実録』元慶3年10月条との記述から、吉備地域を治めた諸氏族、すなわち下道臣・上道臣・ 香屋臣・三野臣・笠臣・苑臣らの実質的な始祖に吉備武彦が位置づけられていたと見られる{{Sfn|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}。また、吉備武彦こそが吉備氏の上祖（始祖）に相当し、記紀において吉備氏の祖とされる[[吉備津彦命]]・稚武彦命（系譜上における吉備武彦の祖父または父）兄弟は、皇室と吉備氏のつながりを設定するために吉備武彦の名を分割して創作された架空の人物であるとする説もある小野里了一「〈吉備臣〉氏の系譜とその実像」加藤謙吉 編『日本古代の王権と地方』（大和書房、2015年） ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7。 そのほか、ヤマトタケル伝説に吉備武彦が参加していることから、この伝説の作成に吉備氏が関与しているという指摘もある{{Sfn|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=吉備武彦|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} {{デフォルトソート:きひ の たけひこ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:吉備氏|たけひこ]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|人きひのたけひこ]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[吉備武彦]] to [[Kibi no Takehiko]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''吉備 武彦'''（きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}）は、『[[日本書紀]]』などに伝わる[[古代日本]]の人物。『日本書紀』では「吉備武彦」、他文献では「吉備武彦命」・「吉備建彦命」とも表記される。 [[景行天皇]]朝における[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]東征の従者の一人とされる{{Sfn|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 系譜 == 吉備武彦の出自について『[[日本書紀]]』に記載はない。『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、左京皇別 下道朝臣条・右京皇別 廬原公条で[[稚武彦命]]（第7代[[孝霊天皇]][[皇子]]）の孫とし、右京皇別 真髪部条では稚武彦命の子とする。 子については、『日本書紀』[[景行天皇]]51年8月4日条において、娘の吉備穴戸武媛が、日本武尊の[[妃]]となって武卵王（たけかいごのきみ）と十城別王（とおきわけのきみ）の2子を産んだと見える{{Sfn|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}。また『日本書紀』[[応神天皇]]22年9月10日条・『[[日本三代実録]]』[[元慶]]3年（[[879年]]）10月22日条では、子として浦凝別（苑臣祖）・[[御友別]]（[[吉備氏|吉備臣]]祖）・[[鴨別]]（笠臣祖）・兄媛（[[応神天皇]]妃）らの名が記されている{{Sfn|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+『[[日本書紀]]』応神天皇22年9月条、『[[日本三代実録]]』元慶3年10月条に基づく関係系図 |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''吉備武彦'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01=浦凝別
［苑臣祖］
（苑県に封）|02=[[御友別]]
［[[吉備氏|吉備臣]]祖］|03=[[鴨別]]
［笠臣祖］
（波区芸県に封）|04={{color|#FC4E6B|兄媛}}
（[[応神天皇]]妃）
（織部を賜う）}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=稲速別
［下道臣祖］
（川島県に封）|02=仲彦
［上道臣・香屋臣祖］
（上道県に封）|03=弟彦
［三野臣祖］
（三野県に封）}} {{familytree/end}} |} == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[景行天皇]]40年7月16日条によると、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の東征にあたって、その従者として吉備武彦と[[大伴武日|大伴武日連]]が付けられたという。また同年の是歳条によると、吉備武彦は途中で[[越国]]に視察のため派遣され、のち日本武尊と美濃で合流した。そののち日本武尊が病を得ると、吉備武彦はその遺言を伝える使者として景行天皇の元に遣わされたという{{Sfn|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 関連して『[[古事記]]』景行天皇段では、吉備武彦と同一人物かは定かでないが、吉備臣らの祖の「'''御鉏友耳建日子'''（みすきともみみたけひこ）」が倭建命（日本武尊）の従者として東征に遣わされたことが記されている{{Sfn|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』右京皇別 廬原公条では、景行天皇の時に稚武彦命の孫の「吉備建彦命」は東方に派遣され、毛人を討って「阿倍廬原国」に到ったのち、天皇に復命した日に廬原国を賜ったとする。 == 後裔 == === 氏族 === 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 下道朝臣 - 吉備朝臣同祖。稚武彦命孫の吉備武彦命の後。 * 右京皇別 真髪部 - 稚武彦命男の吉備武彦命の後。 === 国造 === 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」には、次の[[国造]]が後裔として記載されている。 * [[廬原国造]] *: 志賀高穴穂朝（[[成務天皇]]）の御世に池田坂井君祖の吉備武彦命の子の意加部彦命を国造に定める、という。 *: のちの[[駿河国]][[庵原郡]]庵原郷周辺に推定される『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 八木書店、2013年、p. 176。。 == 考証 == 『日本書紀』応神天皇22年9月条と『日本三代実録』元慶3年10月条との記述から、吉備地域を治めた諸氏族、すなわち下道臣・上道臣・ 香屋臣・三野臣・笠臣・苑臣らの実質的な始祖に吉備武彦が位置づけられていたと見られる{{Sfn|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}。また、吉備武彦こそが吉備氏の上祖（始祖）に相当し、記紀において吉備氏の祖とされる[[吉備津彦命]]・稚武彦命（系譜上における吉備武彦の祖父または父）兄弟は、皇室と吉備氏のつながりを設定するために吉備武彦の名を分割して創作された架空の人物であるとする説もある小野里了一「〈吉備臣〉氏の系譜とその実像」加藤謙吉 編『日本古代の王権と地方』（大和書房、2015年） ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7。 そのほか、ヤマトタケル伝説に吉備武彦が参加していることから、この伝説の作成に吉備氏が関与しているという指摘もある{{Sfn|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=吉備武彦|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|吉備武彦（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} {{デフォルトソート:きひ の たけひこ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:吉備氏|たけひこ]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|人きひのたけひこ]]

'''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient [[Japan]] mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
[Founder of the Enō clan]
(Enō region)|02={{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
[Founder of the Kibi clan]|03={{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
[Founder of the Kasa clan]
(Namikugi region)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Anehime}}
[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko
[Founder of the Shimotsumichi clan]
(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko
[Founder of the Kamitsu clan]
(Kamitsu region)|03=Otoko
[Founder of the Mino clan]
(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連|qq=draft|qq=draft}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} {{citation needed|Kibi no Takehiko}} ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{translated page|ja|吉備武彦|version=100877207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] }} [[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]] [[en:Kibi no Takehiko]]

revision.text:
'''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient [[Japan]] mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Anato Takehime|qq=}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkiwake no Mikoto|qq=}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anehime|qq=}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|qq=}}
[Founder of the Enō clan]
(Enō region)|02={{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|qq=}}
[Founder of the Kibi clan]|03={{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|qq=}}
[Founder of the Kasa clan]
(Namikugi region)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Anehime}}
[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko
[Founder of the Shimotsumichi clan]
(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko
[Founder of the Kamitsu clan]
(Kamitsu region)|03=Otoko
[Founder of the Mino clan]
(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Takehiko|qq=}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Kokuzō|qq=}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Takehiko|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|吉備武彦|version=100877207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] }} [[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]] [[en:Kibi no Takehiko]]

'''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient [[Japan]] mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Anato Takehime|qq=}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkiwake no Mikoto|qq=}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anehime|qq=}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|qq=}}

After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Kokuzō|qq=}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]] and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Takehiko|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|吉備武彦|version=100877207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] }} [[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]] [[en:Kibi no Takehiko]]

revision.text:
'''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient [[Japan]] mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Anato Takehime|qq=}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkiwake no Mikoto|qq=}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anehime|qq=}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|qq=}}
[Founder of the Enō clan]
(Enō region)|02={{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|qq=}}
[Founder of the Kibi clan]|03={{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|qq=}}
[Founder of the Kasa clan]
(Namikugi region)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Anehime}}
[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko
[Founder of the Shimotsumichi clan]
(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko
[Founder of the Kamitsu clan]
(Kamitsu region)|03=Otoko
[Founder of the Mino clan]
(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Takehiko|qq=}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Kokuzō|qq=}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]] and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Takehiko|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|吉備武彦|version=100877207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] }} [[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]] [[en:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]]

It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Kokuzō|qq=}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]] and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Takehiko|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|吉備武彦|version=100877207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] }} [[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]] [[en:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]]

revision.text:
'''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient [[Japan]] mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Anato Takehime|qq=}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkiwake no Mikoto|qq=}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anehime|qq=}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|qq=}}
[Founder of the Enō clan]
(Enō region)|02={{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|qq=}}
[Founder of the Kibi clan]|03={{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|qq=}}
[Founder of the Kasa clan]
(Namikugi region)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Anehime}}
[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko
[Founder of the Shimotsumichi clan]
(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko
[Founder of the Kamitsu clan]
(Kamitsu region)|03=Otoko
[Founder of the Mino clan]
(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Takehiko|qq=}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Kokuzō|qq=}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]] and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Takehiko|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|吉備武彦|version=100877207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] }} [[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]] [[en:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]]

Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anehime|qq=}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|qq=}}
[Founder of the Enō clan]
(Enō region)|02={{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|qq=}}
[Founder of the Kibi clan]|03={{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|qq=}}
[Founder of the Kasa clan]
(Namikugi region)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Anehime}}
[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko
[Founder of the Shimotsumichi clan]
(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko
[Founder of the Kamitsu clan]
(Kamitsu region)|03=Otoko
[Founder of the Mino clan]
(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Takehiko|qq=}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Kokuzō|qq=}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]] and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Takehiko|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|吉備武彦|version=100877207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] }} [[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[ja:吉備武彦]]

'''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient [[Japan]] mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Anato Takehime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkiwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anehime|qq=}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
[Founder of the Enō clan]
(Enō region)|02={{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|qq=}}
[Founder of the Kibi clan]|03={{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|qq=}}
[Founder of the Kasa clan]
(Namikugi region)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Anehime}}
[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko

(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Takehiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Kokuzō|qq=}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]] and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Takehiko|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|吉備武彦|version=100877207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] }} [[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[ja:吉備武彦]]

This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Takehiko|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|吉備武彦|version=100877207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] }} [[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[ja:吉備武彦]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Figures in Japanese Mythology
revision.text:
'''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient [[Japan]] mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Anato Takehime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkiwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anehime|qq=}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
[Founder of the Enō clan]
(Enō region)|02={{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|qq=}}
[Founder of the Kibi clan]|03={{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|qq=}}
[Founder of the Kasa clan]
(Namikugi region)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Anehime}}
[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko
[Founder of the Shimotsumichi clan]
(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko
[Founder of the Kamitsu clan]
(Kamitsu region)|03=Otoko
[Founder of the Mino clan]
(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Takehiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Kokuzō|qq=}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]] and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Takehiko|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|吉備武彦|version=100877207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kibi Clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kibi no Takehiko]] }} [[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[ja:吉備武彦]]

(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Takehiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Kokuzō|qq=}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day [[Suruga Province]]'s [[Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]] and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Takehiko|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|吉備武彦|version=100877207|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kibi no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%90%89%E5%82%99%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[ja:吉備武彦]]

revision.text:
'''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient [[Japan]] mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Anato Takehime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkiwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|qq=}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anehime|qq=}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

[Founder of the Kasa clan]
