[[ja:吉備武彦]] [[ja:吉備武彦]] [[ja:吉備武彦]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Anihime|WD=Q108043809}}; {{ill|Q106382386|WD=Q106382386}}; {{ill|Q18235575|WD=Q18235575}}|father={{ill|Prince Wakatakehiko|WD=Q11596350}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18226257]] Wikidata name: Kibinotakehikonomikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|1=Prince Wakatakehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596350}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Anato Takehime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|1=Miyotowake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18235575}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|1=Kamo Waketakehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226142}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anehime|1=Ehime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119380627}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
[Founder of the Enō clan]
(Enō region)|02={{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|1=Miyotowake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18235575}}
[Founder of the Kibi clan]|03={{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|1=Kamo Waketakehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226142}}
[Founder of the Kasa clan]
(Namikugi region)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Anehime}}
[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko
[Founder of the Shimotsumichi clan]
(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko
[Founder of the Kamitsu clan]
(Kamitsu region)|03=Otoko
[Founder of the Mino clan]
(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[:en:Sendai Kuji Hongi|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[:en:Kokuzō Hongi|Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Kokuzō|1=Ihohara Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486465}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province}}'s [[:en:Ihara District|Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Takehiko|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|吉備武彦|version=100877207|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:吉備武彦]] {{wikidata link|Q18226257}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

revision.text:
[[ja:吉備武彦]] [[ja:吉備武彦]] [[ja:吉備武彦]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Anihime|WD=Q108043809}}; {{ill|Q106382386|WD=Q106382386}}; {{ill|Q18235575|WD=Q18235575}}|father={{ill|Prince Wakatakehiko|WD=Q11596350}}}} '''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|1=Prince Wakatakehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596350}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Anato Takehime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|1=Miyotowake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18235575}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|1=Kamo Waketakehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226142}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anehime|1=Ehime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119380627}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
[Founder of the Enō clan]
(Enō region)|02={{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|1=Miyotowake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18235575}}
[Founder of the Kibi clan]|03={{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|1=Kamo Waketakehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226142}}
[Founder of the Kasa clan]
(Namikugi region)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Anehime}}
[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko
[Founder of the Shimotsumichi clan]
(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko
[Founder of the Kamitsu clan]
(Kamitsu region)|03=Otoko
[Founder of the Mino clan]
(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[:en:Sendai Kuji Hongi|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[:en:Kokuzō Hongi|Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Kokuzō|1=Ihohara Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486465}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province}}'s [[:en:Ihara District|Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Takehiko|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|吉備武彦|version=100877207|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:吉備武彦]] {{wikidata link|Q18226257}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

[Founder of the Kibi clan]|03={{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|1=Q18226142|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226142}}

(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko
[Founder of the Kamitsu clan]
(Kamitsu region)|03=Otoko
[Founder of the Mino clan]
(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[:en:Sendai Kuji Hongi|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[:en:Kokuzō Hongi|Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Kokuzō|1=Ihohara Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486465}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province|1=Suruga Province}}'s [[:en:Ihara District|Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Takehiko|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|吉備武彦|version=100877207|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:吉備武彦]] {{wikidata link|Q18226257}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:吉備武彦]] [[ja:吉備武彦]] [[ja:吉備武彦]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Anihime|WD=Q108043809|1=Anihime}}; {{ill|Q106382386|WD=Q106382386|1=Kibi no Anadotakehime}}; {{ill|Q18235575|WD=Q18235575|1=Miyotowake}}|father={{ill|Prince Wakatakehiko|WD=Q11596350|1=Wakatakehiko}}}} '''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|1=Wakatakehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596350}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Anato Takehime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|1=Miyotowake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18235575}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|1=Q18226142|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226142}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anehime|1=Ehime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119380627}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Takehiko|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|吉備武彦|version=100877207|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:吉備武彦]] {{wikidata link|Q18226257}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

[[ja:吉備武彦]] [[ja:吉備武彦]] [[ja:吉備武彦]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Anihime|WD=Q108043809|1=Anihime}}; {{ill|Q106382386|WD=Q106382386|1=Kibi no Anadotakehime}}; {{ill|Q18235575|WD=Q18235575|1=Miyotowake}}|father={{ill|Prince Wakatakehiko|WD=Q11596350|1=Wakatakehiko}}}} '''Kibi no Takehiko''' (吉備 武彦, きび の たけひこ{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was a figure from ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} mentioned in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other texts. He is referred to as "Kibi no Takehiko" in the ''Nihon Shoki'' and as "Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto" or "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" in other sources. He is known as one of the attendants who accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} during his eastern expedition under the reign of Emperor [[Keikō|Keikō Tennō]]{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Genealogy == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Kibi no Takehiko's origins. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' identifies him as a grandson of {{ill|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|ja|稚武彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|1=Wakatakehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596350}}, a prince of Emperor [[Kōrei|Kōrei Tennō]]. In some entries, he is listed as a son of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. Regarding his descendants, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 51, Eighth Month, Fourth Day entry) records his daughter {{ill|Kibi Anato Takehime|ja|吉備穴戸武媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Anato Takehime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a consort of Yamato Takeru. Together, they had two sons: {{ill|Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja|武卵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekaigo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja|十城別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkiwake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additionally, the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22, Ninth Month, Tenth Day entry) and the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (Engi 3, 879 CE) list other children, including {{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founder of the Enō clan), {{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|1=Miyotowake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18235575}} (founder of the Kibi clan), {{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|1=Q18226142|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226142}} (founder of the Kasa clan), and {{ill|Anehime|ja|兄媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anehime|1=Ehime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119380627}} (consort of Ōjin Tennō). {| class="wikitable" style="margin:0 auto;background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3) |{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01='''Kibi no Takehiko'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||)|-|v|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|||||||03|04| 01={{ill|Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja|浦凝別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urakogori Waketakehiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
[Founder of the Enō clan]
(Enō region)|02={{ill|Mitomo Waketakehiko|ja|御友別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitomo Waketakehiko|1=Miyotowake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18235575}}
[Founder of the Kibi clan]|03={{ill|Kamo Waketakehiko|ja|鴨別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Waketakehiko|1=Q18226142|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18226142}}
[Founder of the Kasa clan]
(Namikugi region)|04={{color|#FC4E6B|Anehime}}
[Consort of Ōjin Tennō]
(Weaving rites)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|v|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01=Inahaya Waketakehiko
[Founder of the Shimotsumichi clan]
(Kawashima region)|02=Nakahiko
[Founder of the Kamitsu clan]
(Kamitsu region)|03=Otoko
[Founder of the Mino clan]
(Mino region)}} {{familytree/end}} |} == Records == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Keikō Tennō, Year 40, Seventh Month, Sixteenth Day entry), Kibi no Takehiko and {{ill|Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Takehiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} accompanied Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. Later that year, Kibi no Takehiko was dispatched to oversee the Echizen region before rejoining Yamato Takeru in Mino. After Yamato Takeru fell ill, Kibi no Takehiko carried his final message to Emperor Keikō{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Keikō Tennō's account), a figure identified as "Misuki Tomomimi Takehiko" (御鉏友耳建日子) was a companion of Yamato Takeru during his eastern expedition. It is unclear whether this refers to Kibi no Takehiko{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Additionally, the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' (Right Capital, Imperial Line: Luhara clan) records that during Keikō Tennō's reign, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named "Kibi Tatehiko no Mikoto" was sent eastward to subdue the barbarians. After completing his mission in the Abe-Luhara region, he returned victorious and was granted the land. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' lists the following clans as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descended from Wakatakehiko no Mikoto through Kibi no Takehiko. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[:en:Sendai Kuji Hongi|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("[[:en:Kokuzō Hongi|Kokuzō Hongi]]") lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') as descendants of Kibi no Takehiko: * {{ill|Luhara Kokuzō|ja|廬原国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Luhara Kokuzō|1=Ihohara Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486465}} *: Under Emperor Seimu (志賀高穴穂朝), Kibi no Takehiko’s son Okabehiko no Mikoto was appointed governor of Luhara Province. This corresponds to modern-day {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province|1=Suruga Province}}'s [[:en:Ihara District|Ihara District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 176.. == Interpretation == Based on the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Ōjin Tennō, Year 22) and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Engi 3), Kibi no Takehiko was positioned as the founder of various clans governing the Kibi region. This includes the Shimotsumichi, Kamitsu, Mino, and Kasa clans, as well as the Enō clan{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. Scholars suggest Kibi no Takehiko is the true ancestor of the Kibi clan and that figures such as {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}} and Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, attributed as his father or grandfather, were later constructs to align the Kibi lineage with the imperial familyRyoichi Onosato, "Genealogy and Reality of the Kibi Clan," in Kiyoshi Kato (Ed.), ''Japanese Ancient Kingship and the Provinces'' (Yamato Shobo, 2015), ISBN 978-4-479-84081-7.. Kibi no Takehiko's participation in Yamato Takeru's legend also suggests the Kibi clan's involvement in shaping this mythology{{Sfn|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Kibi no Takehiko|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kibi no Takehiko (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} ==See Also== * [[Miyotowake]] * [[Sanage Shrine]] * [[Wakatakehiko]] * [[Chiryu Shrine]] * {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Takehiko|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|吉備武彦|version=100877207|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:吉備武彦]] {{wikidata link|Q18226257}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|People]]
